content_en:
Ali ibn Umar ad-Daraqutni (306–385 AH / 918–995 CE) was one of the greatest hadith scholars of the fourth Islamic century, widely regarded as the foremost hafiz (memorizer of hadith) and hadith critic of his era. He was born and based in Baghdad, taking his nisba (attributive name) from Dar al-Qutn, a neighborhood in Baghdad where he grew up. He studied under hundreds of scholars in Iraq, Syria, Egypt, and the Hejaz, and his reputation for encyclopedic knowledge of hadith texts and chains was acknowledged by contemporaries across the Muslim world.

Ad-Daraqutni's most celebrated works reflect two distinct aspects of his mastery. On one hand, his al-Ilal al-Waridah fi al-Ahadith an-Nabawiyyah (The Hidden Defects Present in Prophetic Hadiths) is the supreme work on the identification of hidden defects (ilal) in hadith chains — a level of hadith criticism requiring the most sophisticated analytical ability and the most comprehensive memory. On the other hand, his Sunan ad-Daraqutni is a collection of hadiths organized by legal topic, focused specifically on narrations relevant to jurisprudential disputes.

The Sunan of ad-Daraqutni is distinctive in the hadith literature for its explicit attention to the hadith controversies surrounding specific legal disputes. Ad-Daraqutni did not compile a collection of only sound hadiths, as al-Bukhari and Muslim had done. Rather, he compiled hadiths — both strong and weak — that were relevant to legal debates, explicitly noting the chains of each narration and often commenting on their reliability. This makes the Sunan a uniquely valuable resource for understanding the hadith evidence on both sides of jurisprudential controversies.

The collection contains approximately 4,700 hadiths. It has been printed with commentary and is frequently consulted by scholars researching the hadith basis for specific legal rulings. Understanding its nature — a collection organized around legal controversies rather than a straightforward compilation of authenticated narrations — is essential for using it correctly.
content_id:
Ali ibn Umar al-Daraqutni (306–385 H / 918–995 M) adalah salah satu ulama hadis terbesar pada abad keempat Hijriah, yang secara luas dipandang sebagai hafiz (penghafal hadis) dan kritikus hadis terkemuka di zamannya. Beliau lahir dan menetap di Baghdad, mengambil nisbah (nama atributif) dari Dar al-Qutn, sebuah kawasan di Baghdad tempat beliau dibesarkan. Beliau belajar dari ratusan ulama di Irak, Suriah, Mesir, dan Hijaz, dan reputasinya atas pengetahuan ensiklopedis mengenai teks dan sanad hadis diakui oleh para sezaman di seluruh dunia Muslim.

Karya-karya al-Daraqutni yang paling terkenal mencerminkan dua aspek berbeda dari penguasaannya. Di satu sisi, al-Ilal al-Waridah fi al-Ahadith an-Nabawiyyah (Cacat-cacat Tersembunyi dalam Hadis-hadis Nabawi) merupakan karya tertinggi dalam mengidentifikasi cacat tersembunyi (ilal) dalam sanad hadis — sebuah tingkat kritik hadis yang memerlukan kemampuan analitis paling canggih dan ingatan paling komprehensif. Di sisi lain, Sunan al-Daraqutni adalah kumpulan hadis yang disusun berdasarkan topik hukum, difokuskan khusus pada narasi-narasi yang relevan dengan perselisihan yurisprudensial.

Sunan al-Daraqutni bersifat khas dalam literatur hadis karena perhatiannya yang eksplisit terhadap kontroversi hadis seputar perselisihan hukum tertentu. Al-Daraqutni tidak menyusun kumpulan yang hanya berisi hadis sahih, sebagaimana yang telah dilakukan al-Bukhari dan Muslim. Sebaliknya, beliau menghimpun hadis-hadis — baik yang kuat maupun yang lemah — yang relevan dengan perdebatan hukum, dengan secara eksplisit mencatat sanad setiap narasi dan sering mengomentari keandalannya. Hal ini menjadikan Sunan sebagai sumber yang sangat berharga untuk memahami bukti hadis dari kedua sisi kontroversi yurisprudensial.

Koleksi ini mengandung sekitar 4.700 hadis. Kitab ini telah dicetak disertai komentar dan sering dikonsultasikan oleh para ulama yang meneliti landasan hadis untuk hukum-hukum tertentu. Memahami sifatnya — sebuah koleksi yang disusun di sekitar kontroversi hukum daripada kompilasi narasi-narasi yang telah diautentikasi secara langsung — sangat penting untuk menggunakannya dengan benar.
content_ar: علي ibn عمر ad-Daraqutni (306–385 AH / 918–995 CE) was one of the greatest الحديث العلماء of the رابعاً Islamic century, widely regarded as the foremost hafiz (memorizer of الحديث) and الحديث critic of his era. He was born and based in Baghdad, taking his nisba (attributive name) from Dar al-Qutn, a neighborhood in Baghdad where he grew up. He studied under hundreds of العلماء in Iraq, Syria, Egypt, and the Hejaz, and his reputation for encyclopedic العلم of الحديث texts and chains was acknowledged by contemporaries across the مسلم الدنيا. Ad-Daraqutni's most celebrated works reflect two distinct aspects of his mastery. On one hand, his al-Ilal al-Waridah fi al-الأحاديث an-Nabawiyyah (The Hidden Defects Present in Prophetic Hadiths) is the supreme work on the identification of hidden defects (ilal) in الحديث chains — a level of الحديث criticism requiring the most sophisticated analytical ability and the most comprehensive memory. من ناحية أخرى, his Sunan ad-Daraqutni is a collection of hadiths organized by legal topic, focused specifically on narrations relevant to jurisprudential disputes. The Sunan of ad-Daraqutni is distinctive in the الحديث literature for its explicit attention to the الحديث controversies surrounding specific legal disputes. Ad-Daraqutni did not compile a collection of only sound hadiths, as البخاري and مسلم had done. Rather, جمع hadiths — both strong and weak — that were relevant to legal debates, explicitly noting the chains of each narration and often commenting on their reliability. This makes the Sunan a uniquely valuable resource for الفهم the الحديث evidence on both sides of jurisprudential controversies. The collection contains approximately 4,700 hadiths. It has been printed with commentary and is frequently consulted by العلماء researching the الحديث basis for specific legal rulings. الفهم its nature — a collection organized around legal controversies rather than a straightforward compilation of authenticated narrations — is الجوهري for using it correctly.
